chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْوُقُوفِ بِعَرَفَةَ وَ حَدِّ الْمَوْقِفِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 165 – The pausing at Arafaat and the boundary of the Pausing Station (Al Mowqif)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/122)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/122)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَقَفَ بِعَرَفَاتٍ فَلَمَّا هَمَّتِ الشَّمْسُ أَنْ تَغِيبَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَنْدَفِعَ قَالَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْفَقْرِ وَ مِنْ تَشَتُّتِ الْأَمْرِ وَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَحْدُثُ بِاللَّيْلِ وَ النَّهَارِ أَمْسَى ظُلْمِي مُسْتَجِيراً بِعَفْوِكَ وَ أَمْسَى خَوْفِي مُسْتَجِيراً بِأَمَانِكَ وَ أَمْسَى ذُلِّي مُسْتَجِيراً بِعِزِّكَ وَ أَمْسَى وَجْهِيَ الْفَانِي مُسْتَجِيراً بِوَجْهِكَ الْبَاقِي
يَا خَيْرَ مَنْ سُئِلَ وَ يَا أَجْوَدَ مَنْ أَعْطَى جَلِّلْنِي بِرَحْمَتِكَ وَ أَلْبِسْنِي عَافِيَتَكَ وَ اصْرِفْ عَنِّي شَرَّ جَمِيعِ خَلْقِكَ
قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مَيْمُونٍ وَ سَمِعْتُ أَبِي يَقُولُ يَا خَيْرَ مَنْ سُئِلَ وَ يَا أَوْسَعَ مَنْ أَعْطَى وَ يَا أَرْحَمَ مَنِ اسْتُرْحِمَ ثُمَّ سَلْ حَاجَتَكَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Abdullah Bin Maymoun who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying that Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> paused at Arafaat. So when the sun was about to set, before the rush, said: ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I seek Refuge with You<sup>azwj</sup> from the poverty, and from scattering of the affairs, and from evil of what occurs by the night and the day. I<sup>saww</sup> have come unjust (and) seeking Refuge by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Forgiveness, and have come fearful seeking Refuge by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Security, and have come disgraced seeking Refuge by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Honour, and have come with my<sup>saww</sup> perishing face seeking Refuge by Your<sup>azwj</sup> Everlasting Face.

O the Best One<sup>azwj</sup> to Plead, and O most benevolent of the Granting Ones, Wrap me in Your<sup>azwj</sup> Mercy and Clothe me by Health from You<sup>azwj</sup>, and Exchange the evil of the entirety of the creatures away from me’.

Abdullah Bin Maymoun said, ‘And I heard my father saying, ‘O the Best One<sup>azwj</sup> to Plead to, and O the most Extensive of the ones who give, and O most Merciful of the merciful ones’, then ask your need’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from ‘Abd Allah ibn Maymun who has said the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say this. ‘The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, stayed in ‘Arafat. At about sunset before moving fast said, “O Allah, I seek protection with You against poverty, chaotic affairs and the mischief that takes place during the night and day. It is evening and I seek protection with Your forgiveness against my injustice. It is evening and I seek protection with Your immunity against my fear. It is evening and I seek protection with Your majesty against my lowliness. It is evening and I seek protection with Your ever-living face against my destructible face. O the best One before whom appeals are made, O most generous, lift me up through Your mercy and dress me with Your good health, and keep away from me the evil of all of Your creatures.’” ‘Abd Allah ibn Maymun has said, ‘I heard my father saying, “O the best of those before whom appeals are made, O the most generous of those who grant favors, O the best of those who show kindness and mercy.’ Then ask for your wishes.’”
